subject: Language Arts
description:
Some learners may enter your classroom without having mastered the skills identified in previous grade levels or grade spans. In cases when this occurs, teachers should
  • assess where students are,
  • determine what they need to learn to master the skills appropriate to their grade level or grade span, and
  • access district resources to help students move toward proficiency.
description_plain: If your child needs extra help, the teacher will find out what skills they need and use resources to help them catch up.

subject: Math
description: Classify objects into given categories; count the numbers of objects in each category and sort the categories by count. (Limit category counts to be less than or equal to 10.)
description_plain: Your child can sort objects into groups, count how many are in each group (up to 10), and put the groups in order by size.

subject: Math
description: Directly compare two objects with a measurable attribute in common, to see which object has "more of"/"less of" the attribute and describe the difference.
description_plain: Your child can compare two objects to see which one is longer, heavier, or has more of something and describe the difference.

subject: Math
description: Describe measurable attributes of objects, such as length or weight. Describe several measurable attributes of a single object.
description_plain: Your child can describe things you can measure about objects, like how long or heavy they are, and describe several of these for one object.

subject: Math
description: Describe and compare measurable attributes.
description_plain: Your child can describe and compare things that can be measured, like length or weight.

subject: Math
description: Compose simple shapes to form larger shapes.
description_plain: Your child can put simple shapes together to make bigger shapes.

subject: Math
description: Model shapes in the world by building shapes from components (e.g., sticks and clay balls) and drawing shapes.
description_plain: Your child can build and draw shapes they see around them using parts like sticks and clay balls.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and compare two- and three-dimensional shapes, in different sizes and orientations, using informal language to describe their similarities, differences, parts (e.g., number of sides and vertices/"corners") and other attributes (e.g., having sides of equal length).
description_plain: Your child can look at flat and solid shapes of different sizes and talk about how they are alike and different, including counting sides and corners.

subject: Math
description: Identify shapes as two-dimensional (lying in a plane, "flat") or three-dimensional ("solid").
description_plain: Your child can tell if a shape is flat (2D) or solid (3D).

subject: Math
description: Correctly name shapes regardless of their orientations or overall size.
description_plain: Your child can correctly name shapes no matter how they are turned or how big they are.

subject: Math
description: Describe objects in the environment using names of shapes and describe the relative positions of these objects using terms such as above, below, beside, in front of, behind, and next to.
description_plain: Your child can name shapes of objects around them and describe where they are using words like above, below, beside, in front of, behind, and next to.

subject: Math
description: Identify, describe, analyze, compare, create, and compose shapes based on their attributes.
description_plain: Your child can recognize, describe, compare, create, and put together shapes based on their features.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 5 including zero.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 5, including zero.

subject: Math
description: For any number from 1 to 9, find the number that makes 10 when added to the given number, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record the answer with a drawing or equation.
description_plain: Your child can find the number that adds to another number to make 10, using objects or drawings, and write the answer.

subject: Math
description: Decompose numbers less than or equal to 10 into pairs in more than one way, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record each decomposition by a drawing or equation (e.g., 5 = 2 + 3 and 5 = 4 + 1).
description_plain: Your child can break numbers up to 10 into pairs in different ways using objects or drawings and show each way with a drawing or equation.

subject: Math
description: Solve addition and subtraction word problems, and add and subtract within 10, (e.g., by using objects or drawings to represent the problem).
description_plain: Your child can solve simple addition and subtraction word problems using objects or drawings.

subject: Math
description: Represent addition and subtraction with objects, fingers, mental images, drawings sounds (e.g., claps), acting out situations, verbal explanations, expressions, or equations.
description_plain: Your child can show addition and subtraction using objects, fingers, pictures, sounds, acting, or words.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition as putting together and adding to, and understand subtraction as taking apart and taking from.
description_plain: Your child understands that addition means putting things together and subtraction means taking things apart or away.

subject: Math
description: Compare two numbers between 1 and 10 presented as written numerals.
description_plain: Your child can compare two numbers from 1 to 10 written as numerals to see which is bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: Identify whether the number of objects in one group is greater than, less than, or equal to the number of objects in another group, e.g., by using matching and counting strategies. Include groups with up to ten objects.
description_plain: Your child can tell if one group of up to 10 objects has more, less, or the same number as another group by matching or counting.

subject: Math
description: Compare numbers.
description_plain: Compare numbers.

subject: Math
description: Count to answer "how many?" questions about as many as 20 things arranged in a line, a rectangular array, or a circle, or as many as 10 things in a scattered configuration; given a number from 1-20, count out that many objects.
description_plain: Your child can count up to 20 objects arranged in a line, rectangle, or circle, and up to 10 objects scattered, and can count out a given number of objects up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Understand that each successive number name refers to a quantity that is one larger. Recognize the one more pattern of counting using objects.
description_plain: Your child understands that each number name means one more than the number before it and can show this by counting objects.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the last number name said tells the number of objects counted. The number of objects is the same regardless of their arrangement or the order in which they were counted.
description_plain: Your child knows that the last number said when counting tells how many objects there are, no matter how they are arranged or counted.

subject: Math
description: When counting objects, say the number names in the standard order, pairing each object with one and only one number name and each number name with one and only one object.
description_plain: Your child can count objects by saying number names in order, matching one number to each object.

subject: Math
description: Understand the relationship between numbers and quantities; connect counting to cardinality.
description_plain: Your child understands how numbers and counting relate to the amount of things.

subject: Math
description: Count to tell the number of objects.
description_plain: Your child can count objects to find out how many there are.

subject: Math
description: Write numbers from 0 to 20. Represent a number of objects with a written numeral 0-20 (with 0 representing a count of no objects)
description_plain: Your child can write numbers from 0 to 20 and use those numbers to show how many objects there are, including zero for no objects.

subject: Math
description: Count forward beginning from a given number within the known sequence (instead of having to begin at 1)
description_plain: Your child can count forward starting from any number they know, not just from 1.

subject: Math
description: Count to 100 by ones and by tens
description_plain: Your child can count to 100 by ones and by tens.

subject: Math
description: Know the number names and the count sequence.
description_plain: Your child knows number names and can count in order.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning - Students will look for patterns and rules to help create general methods and shortcuts that can be applied to similar mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child looks for patterns and rules to find easier ways to solve similar math problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure - Students will use their current mathematical understandings to identify patterns and structure to make sense of new learning.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about math to notice patterns and understand new ideas.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision - Students will use precise mathematical language and check their work for accuracy.
description_plain: Your child uses clear math language and checks their work carefully.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically - Students will use math tools such as tables, diagrams, and technology to explore and deepen their understanding of concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses tools like charts, pictures, or technology to explore and understand math ideas.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics - Students will use representations to show their thinking in a variety of ways.
description_plain: Your child shows their math thinking in different ways, like drawings or models.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others - Students will explain their thinking and make sense of the thinking of others.
description_plain: Your child explains their math thinking and listens to others to understand their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively - Students will think about numbers in many ways and make sense of numerical relationships as they solve problems.
description_plain: Your child thinks about numbers in different ways and understands how they relate when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them - Students will plan strategies to use and persevere in solving math problems.
description_plain: Your child plans how to solve math problems and keeps trying until they find the answer.

subject: Math
description: Identify the coins and each corresponding value. (e.g. penny, nickel, dime, and quarter)
description_plain: Your child can identify coins like pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters and know their values.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the hour and half-hour using both analog and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Work with time and money.
description_plain: Work with time and money.

subject: Math
description: Organize, represent, and interpret data with up to three categories; ask and answer questions about the total number of data points, how many in each category, and how many more or less are in one category than in another.
description_plain: Your child can collect and organize data into up to three groups and ask and answer questions about how many are in each group and how the groups compare.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Express the length of an object as a whole number of length units, by laying multiple copies of a shorter object (the length unit) end to end; understand that the length measurement of an object is the number of same-size length units that span it with no gaps or overlaps. Limit to contexts where the object being measured is spanned by a whole number of length units with no gaps or overlaps.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long something is by lining up smaller, same-sized objects end to end without gaps or overlaps.

subject: Math
description: Order three objects by length; compare the lengths of two objects indirectly by using a third object.
description_plain: Your child can put three objects in order from shortest to longest and compare two objects by using a third object.

subject: Math
description: Describe and compare measurable attributes.
description_plain: Your child can describe and compare things that can be measured, like length or weight.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, fourths, and quarters, and use the phrases half of, fourth of, and quarter of. Describe the whole as two of, or four of the shares. Understand for these examples that decomposing into more equal shares creates smaller shares.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into two or four equal parts and use words like halves and quarters to describe them.

subject: Math
description: Compose two-dimensional shapes (rectangles, squares, trapezoids, triangles, half-circles, and quarter-circles) or three-dimensional shapes (cubes, right rectangular prisms, right circular cones, and right circular cylinders) to create a composite shape, and compose new shapes from the composite shape.
description_plain: Your child can put together flat shapes or solid shapes to make new shapes and can also break apart shapes to make others.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish between defining attributes (e.g., triangles are closed and three-sided) versus non-defining attributes (e.g., color, orientation, overall size); build and draw shapes to possess defining attributes.
description_plain: Your child can tell the important features that define shapes, like a triangle having three sides, and can build and draw shapes with those features.

subject: Math
description: Identify, describe, analyze, compare, create, and compose shapes based on their attributes.
description_plain: Your child can recognize, describe, compare, create, and put together shapes based on their features.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in an addition or subtraction equation relating three whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the missing number in an addition or subtraction problem with three numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand the meaning of the equal sign and determine if equations involving addition and subtraction are true or false.
description_plain: Your child understands what the equal sign means and can tell if addition or subtraction equations are true or false.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 20, demonstrating fluency for addition and subtraction within 10. Use strategies such as counting on; making ten (e.g., 8 + 6 = 8 + 2 + 4 = 10 + 4 = 14); decomposing a number leading to a ten (e.g., 13 - 4 = 13 - 3 - 1 = 10 - 1 = 9); using the relationship between addition and subtraction (e.g., knowing that 8 + 4 = 12, one knows 12 - 8 = 4); and creating equivalent but easier or known sums (e.g., adding 6 + 7 by creating the known equivalent 6 + 6 + 1 = 12 + 1 = 13).
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 20 and quickly add and subtract numbers up to 10 using helpful tricks like making ten or breaking numbers apart.

subject: Math
description: Relate counting to addition and subtraction (e.g., by counting on 2 to add 2).
description_plain: Your child can use counting to help add and subtract numbers, like counting on to add.

subject: Math
description: Understand subtraction as an unknown-addend problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve subtraction problems by figuring out what number needs to be added to reach a total.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add.
description_plain: Your child uses math rules to help add numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply properties of operation and the relationship between addition and subtraction within 20.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses math rules and the connection between adding and subtracting numbers up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems that call for addition of three whole numbers whose sum is less than or equal to 20, (e.g., by using objects, drawings, and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.)
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that add three numbers with a total of 20 or less using objects, drawings, or equations with a missing number.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 20 to solve word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, (e.g., by using objects, drawings, and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems with addition and subtraction within 20 by using objects, drawings, or simple equations with a blank for the unknown number.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition as putting together and adding to, and understand subtraction as taking apart and taking from.
description_plain: Your child understands that addition means putting things together and subtraction means taking things apart or away.

subject: Math
description: Subtract multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 from multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 (positive or zero differences), using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can subtract multiples of 10 from each other using drawings or models and explain how they did it.

subject: Math
description: Given a two-digit number, mentally find 10 more or 10 less than the number, without having to count; explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can quickly find 10 more or 10 less than a two-digit number in their head and explain how they know.

subject: Math
description: Add within 100, including adding a two-digit number and a one-digit number, and adding a two-digit number and a multiple of 10, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used. Understand that in adding two-digit numbers, one adds tens and tens, ones and ones; and sometimes it is necessary to compose a ten.
description_plain: Your child can add numbers up to 100, including adding two-digit and one-digit numbers or multiples of 10, using objects or drawings and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
description_plain: Your child uses place value and math rules to add and subtract numbers.

subject: Math
description: Compare two two-digit numbers based on meanings of the tens and ones digits, recording the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, and <.
description_plain: Your child can compare two two-digit numbers by looking at the tens and ones, and use symbols like >, =, and < to show which is bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: The numbers 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90 refer to one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine tens (and 0 ones).
description_plain: Your child understands that numbers like 10, 20, 30, etc., mean groups of tens and zero ones.

subject: Math
description: The numbers from 11 to 19 are composed of a ten and one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine ones.
description_plain: Your child knows that numbers from 11 to 19 are made of one ten and some ones.

subject: Math
description: 10 can be thought of as a bundle of ten ones - called a "ten."
description_plain: Your child understands that 10 ones can be grouped together to make a 'ten.'

subject: Math
description: Understand that the two digits of a two-digit number represent amounts of tens and ones. Understand the following as special cases:
description_plain: Your child understands that a two-digit number shows how many tens and how many ones it has.

subject: Math
description: Understand place value.
description_plain: Understand place value.

subject: Math
description: Count to 120, starting at any number less than 120. In this range, read and write numerals and represent a number of objects with a written numeral.
description_plain: Your child can count, read, and write numbers up to 120, starting from any number less than 120, and match numbers to groups of objects.

subject: Math
description: Extend the counting sequence.
description_plain: Extend the counting sequence.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning - Students will look for patterns and rules to help create general methods and shortcuts that can be applied to similar mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child looks for patterns and rules to find easier ways to solve similar math problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure - Students will use their current mathematical understandings to identify patterns and structure to make sense of new learning.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about math to notice patterns and understand new ideas.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision - Students will use precise mathematical language and check their work for accuracy.
description_plain: Your child uses clear math language and checks their work carefully.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically - Students will use math tools such as tables, diagrams, and technology to explore and deepen their understanding of concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses tools like charts, pictures, or technology to explore and understand math ideas.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics - Students will use representations to show their thinking in a variety of ways.
description_plain: Your child shows their math thinking in different ways, like drawings or models.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others - Students will explain their thinking and make sense of the thinking of others.
description_plain: Your child explains their math thinking and listens to others to understand their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively - Students will think about numbers in many ways and make sense of numerical relationships as they solve problems.
description_plain: Your child thinks about numbers in different ways and understands how they relate when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them - Students will plan strategies to use and persevere in solving math problems.
description_plain: Your child plans how to solve math problems and keeps trying until they find the answer.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies, using $ and ¢ symbols appropriately. Example: If you have 2 dimes and 3 pennies, how many cents do you have?
description_plain: Your child can solve money problems using coins and dollar bills and write amounts with $ and ¢ symbols.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time from analog and digital clocks to the nearest five minutes, using a.m. and p.m.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest five minutes on analog and digital clocks, using a.m. and p.m.

subject: Math
description: Work with time and money.
description_plain: Work with time and money.

subject: Math
description: Represent whole numbers as lengths from 0 on a number line diagram with equally spaced points corresponding to the numbers 0, 1, 2, ..., and represent whole-number sums and differences within 100 on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can show numbers on a number line and use it to add and subtract numbers up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve word problems involving lengths that are given in the same units, e.g., by using drawings (such as drawings of rulers) and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems about lengths using addition and subtraction within 100, with drawings or equations.

subject: Math
description: Relate addition and subtraction to length.
description_plain: Your child can connect adding and subtracting to measuring length.

subject: Math
description: Draw a picture graph and a bar graph (with single-unit scale) to represent a data set with up to four categories. Solve simple put-together, take-apart, and compare problems using information presented in a bar graph.
description_plain: Your child can make picture and bar graphs with up to four categories and use them to solve simple problems.

subject: Math
description: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths of several objects to the nearest whole unit, or by making repeated measurements of the same object. Organize and record data on a line plot, where the horizontal scale is marked off in whole-number units.
description_plain: Your child can measure several objects, record the lengths on a line plot, and organize the data.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Estimate lengths using units of inches, feet, centimeters, and meters.
description_plain: Your child can estimate lengths using inches, feet, centimeters, and meters.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object twice, using length units of different lengths for the two measurements; describe how the two measurements relate to the size of the unit chosen.
description_plain: Your child can measure something twice using different units and explain how the measurements change with the unit size.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object by selecting and using appropriate tools such as rulers, yardsticks, meter sticks, and measuring tapes.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long things are using tools like rulers or measuring tapes.

subject: Math
description: Measure to determine how much longer one object is than another, expressing the length difference in terms of a standard length unit.
description_plain: Your child can measure how much longer one object is than another using standard units.

subject: Math
description: Describe and compare measurable attributes.
description_plain: Your child can describe and compare things that can be measured, like length or weight.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, thirds, half of, a third of, etc., and describe the whole as two halves, three thirds, four fourths. Recognize that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into equal parts like halves, thirds, or fourths and describe these parts and the whole.

subject: Math
description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares and count to find the total number of them.
description_plain: Your child can divide a rectangle into equal squares by making rows and columns and count how many squares there are.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and draw shapes having specified attributes, such as a given number of angles or a given number of equal faces. Identify triangles, quadrilaterals (including squares, rectangles, rhombuses, and trapezoids) pentagons, hexagons, and cubes. Sizes are compared directly or visually, not compared by measuring.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and draw shapes by their features, like number of sides or equal faces, and name shapes like triangles and cubes.

subject: Math
description: Identify, describe, analyze, compare, create, and compose shapes based on their attributes.
description_plain: Your child can recognize, describe, compare, create, and put together shapes based on their features.

subject: Math
description: Use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rectangular arrays with up to 5 rows and up to 5 columns; write an equation to express the total as a sum of equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can use addition to find out how many objects are in a rectangle with up to 5 rows and 5 columns, and can write an equation to show this.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a group of objects (up to 20) has an odd or even number of members, e.g., by pairing objects or counting them by 2s; write an equation to express an even number as a sum of two equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a group of up to 20 objects has an odd or even number by pairing or counting by twos and write an equation showing even numbers as two equal parts.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 20 using mental strategies. By end of Grade 2, know from memory all sums of two one-digit numbers.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 20 in their head and knows all sums of two one-digit numbers by memory.

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply properties of operation and the relationship between addition and subtraction within 20.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses math rules and the connection between adding and subtracting numbers up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve one- and two-step word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve one- and two-step word problems using addition and subtraction within 100, drawing pictures and writing equations with unknown numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition as putting together and adding to, and understand subtraction as taking apart and taking from.
description_plain: Your child understands that addition means putting things together and subtraction means taking things apart or away.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning - Students will look for patterns and rules to help create general methods and shortcuts that can be applied to similar mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child looks for patterns and rules to find easier ways to solve similar math problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure - Students will use their current mathematical understandings to identify patterns and structure to make sense of new learning.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about math to notice patterns and understand new ideas.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision - Students will use precise mathematical language and check their work for accuracy.
description_plain: Your child uses clear math language and checks their work carefully.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically - Students will use math tools such as tables, diagrams, and technology to explore and deepen their understanding of concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses tools like charts, pictures, or technology to explore and understand math ideas.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics - Students will use representations to show their thinking in a variety of ways.
description_plain: Your child shows their math thinking in different ways, like drawings or models.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others - Students will explain their thinking and make sense of the thinking of others.
description_plain: Your child explains their math thinking and listens to others to understand their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively - Students will think about numbers in many ways and make sense of numerical relationships as they solve problems.
description_plain: Your child thinks about numbers in different ways and understands how they relate when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them - Students will plan strategies to use and persevere in solving math problems.
description_plain: Your child plans how to solve math problems and keeps trying until they find the answer.

subject: Math
description: Solve real world and mathematical problems involving perimeters of polygons, including finding the perimeter given the side lengths, finding an unknown side length, and exhibiting rectangles with the same perimeter and different areas or with the same area and different perimeters.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about the perimeter of shapes by adding side lengths, finding missing sides, and comparing rectangles with the same perimeter or area.

subject: Math
description: Recognize area as additive. Find areas of rectilinear figures by decomposing them into non-overlapping rectangles and adding the areas of the non-overlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real world problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of shapes made of rectangles by breaking them apart and adding the areas, and use this to solve real problems.

subject: Math
description: Use tiling to show in a concrete case that the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths a and b + c is the sum of a × b and a × c. Use area models to represent the distributive property in mathematical reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can use small squares to show that the area of a rectangle with sides a and b + c is the same as adding the areas of rectangles with sides a and b, and a and c.

subject: Math
description: Multiply side lengths to find areas of rectangles with whole-number side lengths in the context of solving real world and mathematical problems and represent whole-number products as rectangular areas in mathematical reasoning.
description_plain: Your child multiplies side lengths to find the area of rectangles and uses this to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths by tiling it and show that the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of a rectangle by counting tiles and knows this is the same as multiplying its side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Relate area to the operations of multiplication and addition.
description_plain: Your child understands that area relates to multiplication and addition.

subject: Math
description: Measure areas by counting unit squares (square cm, square m, square in, square ft, and non- standard units
description_plain: Your child can find the area of shapes by counting squares, using standard or non-standard units.

subject: Math
description: A plane figure which can be covered without gaps or overlaps by n unit squares is said to have an area of n square units.
description_plain: Your child knows that if a shape can be covered exactly by n squares, its area is n square units.

subject: Math
description: A square with side length 1 unit, called "a unit square," is said to have "one square unit" of area, and can be used to measure area.
description_plain: Your child understands that a square with sides one unit long is called a unit square and measures one square unit of area.

subject: Math
description: Recognize area as an attribute of plane figures and understand concepts of area measurement.
description_plain: Your child knows that area is a property of flat shapes and understands what it means to measure area.

subject: Math
description: Understand concepts of Geometric measurement: involving perimeter, area, and volume.
description_plain: Your child can understand how to measure shapes by finding their perimeter, area, and volume.

subject: Math
description: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths of objects using rulers marked with halves and fourths of an inch. Record and show the data by making a line plot, where the horizontal scale is marked off in appropriate units— whole numbers, halves, or fourths.
description_plain: Your child can measure lengths using rulers marked in halves and fourths of an inch and make a line plot to show the measurements.

subject: Math
description: Draw a scaled picture graph and a scaled bar graph to represent a data set with several categories. Solve one- and two-step "how many more" and "how many less" problems using information presented in scaled bar graphs.
description_plain: Your child can draw picture and bar graphs with scales to show data and solve questions about how much more or less one category has compared to another.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Measure and estimate liquid volumes and masses of objects using standard metric units of grams (g), kilograms (kg), and liters (l). Add, subtract, multiply, or divide to solve one-step word problems involving masses or volumes that are given in the same metric units, e.g., by using drawings (such as a beaker with a measurement scale) to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can measure and estimate liquid amounts and object weights using grams, kilograms, and liters, and solve simple problems using these measurements.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest minute and measure time intervals in minutes using analog and digital clocks. Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals in minutes, e.g., by representing the problem on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest minute using clocks and solve problems adding or subtracting time intervals.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving measurement, conversion of measurement and estimation of intervals of time, liquid volumes, and masses of objects.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems involving measuring and converting time, liquid amounts, and weights.

subject: Math
description: Partition shapes into parts with equal areas. Express the area of each part as a unit fraction of the whole.
description_plain: Your child can divide shapes into equal parts and describe each part as a fraction of the whole shape.

subject: Math
description: Understand that shapes in different categories (e.g., rhombuses, rectangles, and others) may share attributes (e.g., having four sides), and that the shared attributes can define a larger category (e.g., quadrilaterals). Recognize rhombuses, rectangles, and squares as examples of quadrilaterals, and draw examples of quadrilaterals that do not belong to any of these subcategories.
description_plain: Your child can recognize that shapes like rhombuses, rectangles, and squares share features like having four sides, and can sort and compare shapes by their sides and angles, including drawing quadrilaterals that don't fit into those groups.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, compare, create, and compose shapes based on their attributes.
description_plain: Your child can look at shapes, compare their features, and create new shapes based on those features.

subject: Math
description: Identify arithmetic patterns (including patterns in the addition table or multiplication table) and explain them using properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain patterns in addition and multiplication tables using math rules.

subject: Math
description: Identify, explain, generate and analyze patterns.
description_plain: Your child can find, explain, create, and study different patterns.

subject: Math
description: Solve two-step word problems using the four operations. Represent these problems using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity. Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies including rounding.
description_plain: Your child can solve two-step word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division and check their answers by estimating.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving the four operations.
description_plain: Your child can solve math problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply and divide within 100, using strategies such as the relationship between multiplication and division (e.g., knowing that 8 × 5 = 40, one knows 40 ÷ 5 = 8) or properties of operations. By the end of Grade 3, know from memory all products of two one-digit numbers.
description_plain: Your child can quickly multiply and divide numbers up to 100 using strategies like knowing related facts and math properties, and knows all multiplication and division facts for single-digit numbers by memory.

subject: Math
description: Understand division as an unknown-factor problem.
description_plain: Your child understands division as finding an unknown number that, when multiplied, gives the original number.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to multiply.
description_plain: Your child can use rules about numbers to help multiply more easily.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in a multiplication or division equation relating three whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can find the missing number in multiplication or division problems without needing special math words.

subject: Math
description: Use multiplication and division within 100 to solve word problems in situations involving equal groups, arrays, and measurement quantities, e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems about equal groups, arrays, or measurements using multiplication and division, and write equations with a letter for the unknown.

subject: Math
description: Interpret whole-number quotients of whole numbers, e.g., interpret 56 ÷ 8 as the number of objects in each share when 56 objects are partitioned equally into 8 shares, or as a number of shares when 56 objects are partitioned into equal shares of 8 objects each.
description_plain: Your child can explain division problems like 56 ÷ 8 by showing how many objects are in each group or how many groups there are.

subject: Math
description: Interpret products of whole numbers, e.g., interpret 5 × 7 as the total number of objects in 5 groups of 7 objects each.
description_plain: Your child can understand multiplication as putting equal groups together, like seeing 5 × 7 as 5 groups of 7 objects each.

subject: Math
description: Understand properties of multiplication and the relationship between multiplication and division to represent and solve problems within 100.
description_plain: Your child understands how multiplication and division are connected and can use this to solve problems with numbers up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with the same numerator or the same denominator by reasoning about their size. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
description_plain: Your child compares two fractions with the same top or bottom number by thinking about their size and uses >, =, or < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Express whole numbers as fractions and recognize fractions that are equivalent to whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write whole numbers as fractions and recognize when fractions equal whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and generate simple equivalent fractions, e.g., ½ = 2/4, 4/6 = 2/3. Explain why the fractions are equivalent, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and create simple equivalent fractions like 1/2 = 2/4 and explain why they are equal using pictures.

subject: Math
description: Understand two fractions as equivalent (equal) if they are the same size, or the same point on a number line.
description_plain: Your child understands that two fractions are equal if they represent the same amount or point on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Explain equivalence of fractions in special cases, and compare fractions by reasoning about their size.
description_plain: Your child can explain when two fractions are equal and compare fractions by thinking about their size.

subject: Math
description: Represent a fraction a/b on a number line diagram by marking off a lengths 1/b from 0. Recognize that the resulting interval has size a/b and that its endpoint locates the number a/b on the number line.
description_plain: Your child can show a fraction on a number line by marking equal parts and understands where the fraction fits on the line.

subject: Math
description: Represent a fraction 1/b on a number line diagram by defining the interval from 0 to 1 as the whole and partitioning it into b equal parts. Recognize that each part has size 1/b and that the endpoint of the part based at 0 locates the number 1/b on the number line.
description_plain: Your child can show the fraction 1/b on a number line by dividing the space from 0 to 1 into equal parts and identifying one part.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction as a number on the number line; represent fractions on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child understands fractions as numbers and can place them correctly on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Understand a unit fraction 1/b as the quantity formed by 1 part when a whole is partitioned into b equal parts; understand a fraction a/b as the quantity formed by a parts of size 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction like 1/b means one part out of b equal parts, and a/b means a parts of size 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Develop and extend the understanding of fractions as numbers, including equivalence and ordering.
description_plain: Your child can understand fractions better by learning when fractions are equal and how to order them.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning - Students will look for patterns and rules to help create general methods and shortcuts that can be applied to similar mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child looks for patterns and rules to find easier ways to solve similar math problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure - Students will use their current mathematical understandings to identify patterns and structure to make sense of new learning.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about math to notice patterns and understand new ideas.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision - Students will use precise mathematical language and check their work for accuracy.
description_plain: Your child uses clear math language and checks their work carefully.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically - Students will use math tools such as tables, diagrams, and technology to explore and deepen their understanding of concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses tools like charts, pictures, or technology to explore and understand math ideas.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics - Students will use representations to show their thinking in a variety of ways.
description_plain: Your child shows their math thinking in different ways, like drawings or models.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others - Students will explain their thinking and make sense of the thinking of others.
description_plain: Your child explains their math thinking and listens to others to understand their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively - Students will think about numbers in many ways and make sense of numerical relationships as they solve problems.
description_plain: Your child thinks about numbers in different ways and understands how they relate when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them - Students will plan strategies to use and persevere in solving math problems.
description_plain: Your child plans how to solve math problems and keeps trying until they find the answer.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angle measure as additive. When an angle is decomposed into non-overlapping parts, the angle measure of the whole is the sum of the angle measures of the parts. Solve addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram in real world and mathematical problems, e.g., by using an equation with a symbol for the unknown angle measure.
description_plain: Your child can find the size of a whole angle by adding the sizes of its parts and solve problems to find missing angles using addition or subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. Sketch angles of specified measure.
description_plain: Your child can measure angles using a protractor and draw angles with a given size.

subject: Math
description: An angle that turns through n one-degree angles is said to have an angle measure of n degrees.
description_plain: Your child knows that an angle made by turning through n one-degree steps measures n degrees.

subject: Math
description: An angle is measured with reference to a circle with its center at the common endpoint of the rays, by considering the fraction of the circular arc between the points where the two rays intersect the circle. An angle that turns through 1/360 of a circle is called a "one-degree angle," and can be used to measure angles.
description_plain: Your child learns that angles are measured by how much they turn around a circle, with one degree being 1/360 of a full turn.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angles as geometric shapes that are formed wherever two rays share a common endpoint, and understand concepts of angle measurement:
description_plain: Your child knows that angles are shapes made by two rays sharing a point and learns how to measure them.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concept of angle and measure angles.
description_plain: Your child understands what an angle is and can measure angles.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (½, ¼, 1/8). Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements using fractions like 1/2 or 1/4 and solve addition or subtraction problems using the plot.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two-dimensional figure as a line across the figure such that the figure can be folded along the line into matching parts. Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry.
description_plain: Your child can find and draw lines of symmetry on flat shapes by folding them to see if both sides match.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines, or the presence or absence of angles of a specified size. Recognize right triangles as a category and identify right triangles.
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes by looking for parallel or perpendicular lines and by naming triangles as right, acute, or obtuse based on their angles.

subject: Math
description: Draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse), and perpendicular and parallel lines. Identify these in two-dimensional figures.
description_plain: Your child can draw points, lines, line segments, rays, and different types of angles, and find these parts in flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Draw and identify lines and angles and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
description_plain: Your child can draw and recognize different lines and angles and group shapes based on these features.

subject: Math
description: Generate a number or shape pattern that follows a given rule. Identify apparent features of the pattern that were not explicit in the rule itself.
description_plain: Your child can create number or shape patterns that follow a rule and notice new features in the pattern that weren’t obvious from the rule.

subject: Math
description: Identify, explain, generate and analyze patterns.
description_plain: Your child can find, explain, create, and study different patterns.

subject: Math
description: Find all factor pairs for a whole number in the range 1-100. Recognize that a whole number is a multiple of each of its factors. Determine whether a given whole number in the range 1-100 is a multiple of a given one-digit number. Determine whether a given whole number in the range 1-100 is prime or composite.
description_plain: Your child can find all factor pairs of numbers from 1 to 100, know when a number is a multiple of another, and tell if a number is prime or composite.

subject: Math
description: Gain familiarity with factors and multiples.
description_plain: Your child becomes familiar with factors and multiples of numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve multistep word problems posed with whole numbers and having whole-number answers using the four operations, including problems in which remainders must be interpreted. Represent these problems using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity. Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies including rounding.
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, including problems with remainders, and write equations with letters for unknowns, checking if answers make sense by estimating.

subject: Math
description: Multiply or divide to solve word problems involving multiplicative comparison, e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem, distinguishing multiplicative comparison from additive comparison.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that compare amounts by multiplying or dividing, using drawings and equations with symbols for unknown numbers.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a multiplication equation as a comparison, e.g., interpret 35 = 5 × 7 as a statement that 35 is 5 times as many as 7 and 7 times as many as 5. Represent verbal statements of multiplicative comparisons as multiplication equations.
description_plain: Your child can explain multiplication as comparing numbers, like saying 35 is 5 times as many as 7, and can write multiplication equations from words.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving the four operations.
description_plain: Your child can solve math problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction or whole number by a fraction.
description_plain: Your child can multiply fractions and whole numbers by fractions, building on what they already know about multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving multiplication of a fraction by a whole number, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that multiply a fraction by a whole number using pictures and equations.

subject: Math
description: Understand a multiple of a/b as a multiple of 1/b and use this understanding to multiply a fraction by a whole number.
description_plain: Your child understands how to multiply a fraction by a whole number by thinking about multiples of parts of the fraction.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction a/b as a multiple of 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands a fraction like a/b as a number made up of a parts of size 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction by a whole number.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a fraction by a whole number and solve real-life problems using this skill.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to multiply and divide fractions.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide fractions using what they already know about multiplication and division.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole and having like denominators, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that add or subtract fractions with the same denominator using drawings or fraction models.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract mixed numbers with like denominators, e.g., by replacing each mixed number with an equivalent fraction, and/or by using properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract mixed numbers with the same bottom number by changing them into improper fractions or using addition and subtraction rules.

subject: Math
description: Decompose a fraction into a sum of fractions with the same denominator in more than one way, recording each decomposition by an equation. Justify decompositions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model to build fractions from unit fractions.
description_plain: Your child can break a fraction into smaller fractions with the same bottom number in different ways and explain their thinking using drawings or models.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition and subtraction of fractions as joining and separating parts referring to the same whole.
description_plain: Your child understands that adding and subtracting fractions means putting parts together or taking parts apart from the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction a/b with a > 1 as a sum of fractions 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction like a/b (where a is bigger than 1) is the same as adding several 1/b fractions.

subject: Math
description: Use equivalent fractions as a strategy to add and subtract fractions.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions by using equivalent fractions.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to hundredths by reasoning about their size. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two decimals refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual model.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals to hundredths by thinking about their size, use >, =, or < to show which is bigger, and explain why, making sure the decimals refer to the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Use decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 or 100.
description_plain: Your child can write fractions with denominators 10 or 100 as decimals.

subject: Math
description: Express a fraction with denominator 10 as an equivalent fraction with denominator 100 and use this technique to add two fractions with respective denominators 10 and 100.2.
description_plain: Your child can change a fraction with denominator 10 to an equivalent fraction with denominator 100 and use this to add fractions with denominators 10 and 100.

subject: Math
description: Understand decimal notation for fractions, and compare decimal fractions.
description_plain: Your child understands decimals as another way to write fractions and can compare decimal numbers.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators, e.g., by creating common denominators or numerators, or by comparing to a benchmark fraction such as ½. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions with different top and bottom numbers by finding common denominators or numerators, or by comparing to ½, and can use >, =, or < to show which is bigger, explaining their answer with pictures.

subject: Math
description: Explain why a fraction a/b is equivalent to a fraction (n × a)/(n × b) by using visual fraction models, with attention to how the number and size of the parts differ even though the two fractions themselves are the same size. Use this principle to recognize and generate equivalent fractions, including fractions greater than 1.
description_plain: Your child can explain why two fractions are equal by using pictures to show how the parts are different sizes but the fractions are the same, and can find and create equivalent fractions, including those greater than 1.

subject: Math
description: Develop and extend the understanding of fractions as numbers, including equivalence and ordering.
description_plain: Your child can understand fractions better by learning when fractions are equal and how to order them.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning - Students will look for patterns and rules to help create general methods and shortcuts that can be applied to similar mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child looks for patterns and rules to find easier ways to solve similar math problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure - Students will use their current mathematical understandings to identify patterns and structure to make sense of new learning.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about math to notice patterns and understand new ideas.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision - Students will use precise mathematical language and check their work for accuracy.
description_plain: Your child uses clear math language and checks their work carefully.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically - Students will use math tools such as tables, diagrams, and technology to explore and deepen their understanding of concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses tools like charts, pictures, or technology to explore and understand math ideas.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics - Students will use representations to show their thinking in a variety of ways.
description_plain: Your child shows their math thinking in different ways, like drawings or models.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others - Students will explain their thinking and make sense of the thinking of others.
description_plain: Your child explains their math thinking and listens to others to understand their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively - Students will think about numbers in many ways and make sense of numerical relationships as they solve problems.
description_plain: Your child thinks about numbers in different ways and understands how they relate when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them - Students will plan strategies to use and persevere in solving math problems.
description_plain: Your child plans how to solve math problems and keeps trying until they find the answer.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (½, ¼, 1/8). Use operations on fractions for this grade to solve problems involving information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements in fractions like ½, ¼, or 1/8 and use fraction operations to solve problems from the plot.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Represent real world and mathematical problems by graphing points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane and interpret coordinate values of points in the context of the situation.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on the first part of a coordinate grid and explain what the points mean in real situations.

subject: Math
description: Use a pair of perpendicular number lines, called axes, to define a coordinate system, with the intersection of the lines (the origin) arranged to coincide with the 0 on each line and a given point in the plane located by using an ordered pair of numbers, called its coordinates. Understand that the first number indicates how far to travel from the origin in the direction of one axis, and the second number indicates how far to travel in the direction of the second axis, with the convention that the names of the two axes and the coordinates correspond (e.g., x-axis and x-coordinate, y-axis and y-coordinate).
description_plain: Your child can use two number lines that cross at zero to find points on a grid by using pairs of numbers that tell how far to go along each line.

subject: Math
description: Graph points on the coordinate plane to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on a graph to help solve real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on properties. (e.g., all rectangles are parallelograms, because they are all quadrilaterals with two pairs of opposite sides parallel.)
description_plain: Your child can group flat shapes into categories based on their properties, like knowing all rectangles are parallelograms.

subject: Math
description: Understand that attributes belonging to a category of two-dimensional figures also belong to all subcategories of that category.
description_plain: Your child understands that if a shape has certain features, then all shapes in smaller groups within that category also have those features.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, compare, create, and compose shapes based on their attributes.
description_plain: Your child can look at shapes, compare their features, and create new shapes based on those features.

subject: Math
description: Generate two numerical patterns using two given rules. Identify apparent relationships between corresponding terms. Form ordered pairs consisting of corresponding terms from the two patterns and graph the ordered pairs on a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can create two number patterns from two rules, find relationships between the numbers in the patterns, make pairs from matching numbers, and graph these pairs on a coordinate plane.

subject: Math
description: Identify, explain, generate and analyze patterns.
description_plain: Your child can find, explain, create, and study different patterns.

subject: Math
description: Write simple expressions that record calculations with numbers and interpret numerical expressions without evaluating them.
description_plain: Your child can write simple math expressions to show calculations and understand what those expressions mean without solving them.

subject: Math
description: Use parentheses, brackets, or braces in numerical expressions, and evaluate expressions with these symbols.
description_plain: Your child can use parentheses, brackets, or braces in math expressions and solve problems that use these symbols.

subject: Math
description: Write and interpret numerical expressions.
description_plain: Your child can write and understand math expressions using numbers and symbols.

subject: Math
description: Solve real world problems involving division of unit fractions by non-zero whole numbers and division of whole numbers by unit fractions, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems where unit fractions are divided by whole numbers or whole numbers are divided by unit fractions, using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a whole number by a unit fraction and compute such quotients.
description_plain: Your child can understand and calculate what happens when a whole number is divided by a fraction.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a unit fraction by a non-zero whole number and compute such quotients.
description_plain: Your child can understand and calculate what happens when a fraction is divided by a whole number.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of division to divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions.1
description_plain: Your child can divide fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by fractions, and solve real-life problems using these skills.

subject: Math
description: Solve real world problems involving multiplication of fractions and mixed numbers, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child solves real-world problems by multiplying fractions and mixed numbers using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction greater than 1 results in a product greater than the given number (recognizing multiplication by whole numbers greater than 1 as a familiar case); explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction less than 1 results in a product smaller than the given number; and relating the principle of fraction equivalence a/b = (n × a)/(n × b) to the effect of multiplying a/b by 1.
description_plain: Your child can explain why multiplying by a fraction greater than 1 makes a number bigger and why multiplying by a fraction less than 1 makes it smaller, and understand how multiplying by 1 keeps a fraction the same.

subject: Math
description: Comparing the size of a product to the size of one factor on the basis of the size of the other factor, without performing the indicated multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can compare the size of a multiplication result to one of the numbers being multiplied by looking at the other number, without actually multiplying.

subject: Math
description: Interpret multiplication scaling (resizing), by:
description_plain: Your child can understand how multiplying changes the size of a number or shape.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator (a/b = a ÷ b). Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions or mixed numbers, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction means dividing the top number by the bottom number and can solve word problems that result in fractions or mixed numbers using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to multiply and divide fractions.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide fractions using what they already know about multiplication and division.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning - Students will look for patterns and rules to help create general methods and shortcuts that can be applied to similar mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child looks for patterns and rules to find easier ways to solve similar math problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure - Students will use their current mathematical understandings to identify patterns and structure to make sense of new learning.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about math to notice patterns and understand new ideas.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision - Students will use precise mathematical language and check their work for accuracy.
description_plain: Your child uses clear math language and checks their work carefully.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically - Students will use math tools such as tables, diagrams, and technology to explore and deepen their understanding of concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses tools like charts, pictures, or technology to explore and understand math ideas.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics - Students will use representations to show their thinking in a variety of ways.
description_plain: Your child shows their math thinking in different ways, like drawings or models.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others - Students will explain their thinking and make sense of the thinking of others.
description_plain: Your child explains their math thinking and listens to others to understand their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively - Students will think about numbers in many ways and make sense of numerical relationships as they solve problems.
description_plain: Your child thinks about numbers in different ways and understands how they relate when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them - Students will plan strategies to use and persevere in solving math problems.
description_plain: Your child plans how to solve math problems and keeps trying until they find the answer.

subject: Math
description: Relating the choice of measures of center and variability to the shape of the data distribution and the context in which the data were gathered.
description_plain: Your child can choose the right way to describe data based on how the data looks and where it came from.

subject: Math
description: Calculating quantitative measures of center (median and/or mean) and variability (range and/or interquartile range and/or mean absolute deviation), as well as describing any overall pattern and any striking deviations from the overall pattern with reference to the context in which the data were gathered.
description_plain: Your child can find the middle and spread of data and describe any patterns or unusual points based on the data's context.

subject: Math
description: Describing the nature of the attribute under investigation, including how it was measured and its units of measurement.
description_plain: Your child can describe what is being measured in data, how it was measured, and the units used.

subject: Math
description: Reporting the number of observations.
description_plain: Your child can tell how many observations or data points were collected.

subject: Math
description: Summarize numerical data sets in relation to their context, such as by:
description_plain: Your child can summarize sets of numbers by explaining what they mean in real life.

subject: Math
description: Display numerical data in plots on a number line, including dot plots, histograms, and box plots.
description_plain: Your child can show number data on number lines using dot plots, histograms, and box plots.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that a measure of center for a numerical data set summarizes all of its values with a single number, while a measure of variation describes how its values vary with a single number.
description_plain: Your child understands that one number can show the middle of data, and another number can show how much the data varies.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a set of data collected to answer a statistical question has a distribution which can be described by its center (mean, median and/or mode), spread (range and/or interquartile range), and overall shape.
description_plain: Your child understands that data collected to answer questions can be described by its average, middle value, most common value, range, and overall shape.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a statistical question as one that anticipates variability in the data related to the question and accounts for it in the answers.
description_plain: Your child can recognize questions that expect different answers because the data changes each time.

subject: Math
description: Summarize distribution using measures of center, variability, and graphical displays.
description_plain: Your child can summarize data by finding averages, how spread out the data is, and by using graphs.

subject: Math
description: Represent three-dimensional figures using nets made up of rectangles and triangles and use the nets to find the surface area of these figures. Apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can draw flat patterns (nets) made of rectangles and triangles to build 3D shapes and use these to find the surface area, solving real problems.

subject: Math
description: Draw polygons in the coordinate plane given coordinates for the vertices; use coordinates to find the length of a side joining points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate. Apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can draw shapes on a grid using points and find side lengths when sides are straight up/down or left/right, solving real problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with fractional edge lengths by packing it with unit cubes of the appropriate unit fraction edge lengths and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths of the prism. Apply the formulas V = l w h and V = B h (where B stands for the area of the base) to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with fractional edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of boxes with fractional side lengths by filling them with small cubes and by multiplying the edges, using formulas in real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of right triangles, other triangles, special quadrilaterals, and polygons by composing into rectangles or decomposing into triangles and other shapes; apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of triangles, special four-sided shapes, and other polygons by breaking them into rectangles or triangles, and use this in real problems.

subject: Math
